Title: Legacy authentication SHALL be blocked
Description: Block legacy authentication to prevent older less secure authentication protocols

Title: Users detected as high risk SHALL be blocked
Description: Block users identified as high risk by Identity Protection

Title: A notification SHOULD be sent to the administrator when high-risk users are detected
Description: Send notification to administrator when Identity Protection detects high-risk users

Title: Sign-ins detected as high risk SHALL be blocked
Description: Block sign-ins identified as high risk by Identity Protection

Title: Phishing-resistant MFA SHALL be enforced for all users
Description: Enforce phishing-resistant multifactor authentication for all users

Title: If phishing-resistant MFA has not been enforced an alternative MFA method SHALL be enforced for all users
Description: Enforce alternative MFA method if phishing-resistant MFA is not yet deployed

Title: If Microsoft Authenticator is enabled it SHALL be configured to show login context information
Description: Configure Microsoft Authenticator to display login context such as application name and location

Title: The Authentication Methods Manage Migration feature SHALL be set to Migration Complete
Description: Set Authentication Methods migration to Migration Complete to use modern auth methods policy

Title: The authentication methods SMS Voice Call and Email OTP SHALL be disabled
Description: Disable SMS Voice Call and Email One-Time Passcode authentication methods

Title: Phishing-resistant MFA SHALL be required for highly privileged roles
Description: Require phishing-resistant MFA for users with highly privileged directory roles

Title: Managed devices SHOULD be required for authentication
Description: Require managed devices as a condition for authentication via conditional access

Title: Managed Devices SHOULD be required to register MFA
Description: Require managed devices for MFA registration to prevent adversary device registration

Title: Device code authentication SHOULD be blocked
Description: Block device code authentication flow to reduce phishing risk

Title: Security logs SHALL be sent to the agency security operations center for monitoring
Description: Send Entra ID security logs to SIEM or SOC for centralized monitoring

Title: Only administrators SHALL be allowed to register applications
Description: Restrict application registration to administrators only

Title: Only administrators SHALL be allowed to consent to applications
Description: Restrict application consent to administrators only

Title: An admin consent workflow SHALL be configured for applications
Description: Configure admin consent workflow so users can request access to apps requiring admin consent

Title: User passwords SHALL NOT expire
Description: Set password expiration policy to not expire per NIST 800-63B guidance

Title: A minimum of two users and a maximum of eight users SHALL be provisioned with the Global Administrator role
Description: Maintain between two and eight Global Administrator role assignments

Title: Privileged users SHALL be provisioned with finer-grained roles instead of Global Administrator
Description: Use specific administrative roles instead of Global Administrator where possible

Title: Privileged users SHALL be provisioned cloud-only accounts separate from on-premises directory
Description: Use cloud-only accounts for privileged roles separate from federated identity providers

Title: Permanent active role assignments SHALL NOT be allowed for highly privileged roles
Description: Require eligible assignments through PIM instead of permanent active assignments for privileged roles

Title: Provisioning users to highly privileged roles SHALL NOT occur outside of a PAM system
Description: Use Privileged Access Management for all highly privileged role provisioning

Title: Activation of the Global Administrator role SHALL require approval
Description: Require approval workflow for Global Administrator role activation in PIM

Title: Eligible and active highly privileged role assignments SHALL trigger an alert
Description: Configure alerts for eligible and active highly privileged role assignments

Title: User activation of the Global Administrator role SHALL trigger an alert
Description: Alert when a user activates the Global Administrator role

Title: User activation of other highly privileged roles SHOULD trigger an alert
Description: Alert when a user activates other highly privileged roles

Title: Guest users SHOULD have limited or restricted access to Entra ID directory objects
Description: Restrict guest user access to directory objects and properties

Title: Only users with the Guest Inviter role SHOULD be able to invite guest users
Description: Restrict guest invitation capability to the Guest Inviter role

Title: Guest invites SHOULD only be allowed to specific external domains authorized by the agency
Description: Restrict guest invitations to approved external domains for legitimate business purposes

Title: Automatic forwarding to external domains SHALL be disabled
Description: Disable automatic email forwarding to external domains to prevent data exfiltration

Title: An SPF policy SHALL be published for each domain that fails all non-approved senders
Description: Publish SPF records that fail unauthorized senders for all agency domains

Title: DKIM SHOULD be enabled for all domains
Description: Enable DomainKeys Identified Mail signing for all agency domains

Title: A DMARC policy SHALL be published for every second-level domain
Description: Publish DMARC DNS records for all second-level domains

Title: The DMARC message rejection option SHALL be p=reject
Description: Set DMARC policy to reject to block spoofed emails

Title: The DMARC point of contact for aggregate reports SHALL include reports@dmarc.cyber.dhs.gov
Description: Include CISA DMARC reporting address in DMARC aggregate report configuration

Title: An agency point of contact SHOULD be included for aggregate and failure reports
Description: Include agency-specific contacts for DMARC aggregate and failure reports

Title: SMTP AUTH SHALL be disabled
Description: Disable SMTP AUTH to prevent legacy authentication for email submission

Title: Contact folders SHALL NOT be shared with all domains
Description: Prevent sharing contact folders with all external domains

Title: Calendar details SHALL NOT be shared with all domains
Description: Prevent sharing calendar details with all external domains

Title: External sender warnings SHALL be implemented
Description: Display warnings to users when emails are received from external senders

Title: A DLP solution SHALL be used
Description: Deploy a Data Loss Prevention solution for Exchange Online

Title: The DLP solution SHALL protect PII and sensitive information as defined by the agency
Description: Configure DLP to protect personally identifiable information and agency-defined sensitive data

Title: The selected DLP solution SHOULD offer services comparable to the native DLP solution
Description: Ensure third-party DLP solutions provide capabilities comparable to Microsoft native DLP

Title: At a minimum the DLP solution SHALL restrict sharing credit card numbers U.S. ITINs and U.S. SSNs via email
Description: Configure DLP to block sharing of credit card numbers ITINs and SSNs through email

Title: Emails SHALL be filtered by attachment file types
Description: Enable attachment file type filtering for inbound emails

Title: The attachment filter SHOULD attempt to determine the true file type and assess file extension
Description: Configure attachment filter to detect true file types beyond file extensions

Title: Disallowed file types SHALL be determined and enforced
Description: Define and enforce a list of disallowed attachment file types

Title: If a third-party filtering solution is used it SHOULD offer services comparable to Microsoft Defender Common Attachment Filter
Description: Ensure third-party attachment filters match Microsoft Defender capabilities

Title: At a minimum click-to-run files SHOULD be blocked
Description: Block click-to-run file types such as exe cmd and vbe at minimum

Title: Emails SHALL be scanned for malware
Description: Enable malware scanning for all inbound and outbound emails

Title: Emails identified as containing malware SHALL be quarantined or dropped
Description: Quarantine or drop emails detected as containing malware

Title: Email scanning SHALL be capable of reviewing emails after delivery
Description: Enable zero-hour auto purge or equivalent post-delivery malware scanning

Title: Impersonation protection checks SHOULD be used
Description: Enable impersonation protection to detect attempts to impersonate users and domains

Title: User warnings comparable to the user safety tips included with EOP SHOULD be displayed
Description: Display user safety tips for suspicious emails comparable to EOP warnings

Title: The phishing protection solution SHOULD include an AI-based phishing detection tool comparable to EOP Mailbox Intelligence
Description: Deploy AI-based phishing detection comparable to Exchange Online Protection Mailbox Intelligence

Title: IP allow lists SHOULD NOT be created
Description: Avoid creating IP allow lists in connection filters to prevent bypassing security checks

Title: Safe lists SHOULD NOT be enabled
Description: Disable safe lists to prevent bypassing spam filtering

Title: Mailbox auditing SHALL be enabled
Description: Enable mailbox auditing to log mailbox access and actions

Title: A spam filter SHALL be enabled
Description: Enable spam filtering for all inbound emails

Title: Spam and high confidence spam SHALL be moved to either the junk email folder or quarantine
Description: Configure spam filter actions to move spam to junk or quarantine

Title: Allowed domains SHALL NOT be added to inbound anti-spam protection policies
Description: Do not add allowed domains to anti-spam policies to prevent bypassing spam filters

Title: If a third-party filtering solution is used the solution SHOULD offer services comparable to native spam filtering
Description: Ensure third-party spam filters provide capabilities comparable to Microsoft native filtering

Title: URL comparison with a block-list SHOULD be enabled
Description: Enable URL block-list comparison for Safe Links protection

Title: Direct download links SHOULD be scanned for malware
Description: Enable scanning of direct download links for malware via Safe Links

Title: User click tracking SHOULD be enabled
Description: Enable tracking of user clicks on URLs for security monitoring

Title: At a minimum required alerts SHALL be enabled for suspicious email activity
Description: Enable alerts for suspicious email sending patterns connector activity forwarding delays and malicious URL clicks

Title: The alerts SHOULD be sent to a monitored address or incorporated into a SIEM
Description: Send email security alerts to a monitored address or SIEM system

Title: Unified Audit logging SHALL be enabled
Description: Enable Microsoft 365 Unified Audit Logging for Exchange Online

Title: Audit logs SHALL be maintained for at least the minimum duration dictated by OMB M-21-31
Description: Retain audit logs for at least the duration required by OMB M-21-31 Appendix C

Title: The standard and strict preset security policies SHALL be enabled
Description: Enable both standard and strict preset security policies in Microsoft Defender

Title: All users SHALL be added to Exchange Online Protection in either the standard or strict preset security policy
Description: Ensure all users are covered by EOP through preset security policies

Title: All users SHALL be added to Defender for Office 365 protection in either the standard or strict preset security policy
Description: Ensure all users are covered by Defender for Office 365 through preset security policies

Title: Sensitive accounts SHALL be added to Exchange Online Protection in the strict preset security policy
Description: Add sensitive accounts to the strict preset security policy for enhanced EOP protection

Title: Sensitive accounts SHALL be added to Defender for Office 365 protection in the strict preset security policy
Description: Add sensitive accounts to the strict preset security policy for enhanced Defender protection

Title: User impersonation protection SHOULD be enabled for sensitive accounts in both preset policies
Description: Enable user impersonation protection for sensitive accounts in standard and strict policies

Title: Domain impersonation protection SHOULD be enabled for domains owned by the agency
Description: Enable domain impersonation protection for agency-owned domains in preset policies

Title: Domain impersonation protection SHOULD be added for key suppliers and partners
Description: Enable domain impersonation protection for key supplier and partner domains in preset policies

Title: Safe attachments SHOULD be enabled for SharePoint OneDrive and Microsoft Teams
Description: Enable Safe Attachments scanning for files in SharePoint OneDrive and Teams

Title: A custom DLP policy SHALL be configured to protect PII and sensitive information
Description: Configure custom DLP policy blocking credit card numbers ITINs and SSNs at minimum

Title: The custom policy SHOULD be applied to Exchange OneDrive SharePoint Teams chat and Devices
Description: Apply DLP custom policy across Exchange OneDrive SharePoint Teams and endpoint devices

Title: The action for the custom policy SHOULD be set to block sharing sensitive information with everyone
Description: Configure DLP policy action to block sharing of sensitive information

Title: Notifications to inform users and help educate them on proper use of sensitive information SHOULD be enabled
Description: Enable DLP policy notifications to educate users about proper handling of sensitive data

Title: A list of apps that are restricted from accessing files protected by DLP policy SHOULD be defined
Description: Define restricted apps that cannot access DLP-protected files

Title: The custom policy SHOULD include an action to block access to sensitive information by restricted apps
Description: Configure DLP to block restricted apps and unwanted Bluetooth applications from accessing sensitive data

Title: At a minimum the alerts required by the CISA EXO baseline SHALL be enabled
Description: Enable all alert policies required by the CISA Exchange Online security baseline

Title: The alerts SHOULD be sent to a monitored address or incorporated into a SIEM
Description: Send Defender alerts to a monitored address or Security Information and Event Management system

Title: Unified Audit logging SHALL be enabled
Description: Enable Microsoft 365 Unified Audit Logging

Title: Audit logs SHALL be maintained for at least the minimum duration dictated by OMB M-21-31
Description: Retain audit logs for at least the duration required by OMB Memorandum M-21-31

Title: External sharing for SharePoint SHALL be limited to Existing guests or Only people in your organization
Description: Restrict SharePoint external sharing to existing guests or internal users only

Title: External sharing for OneDrive SHALL be limited to Existing guests or Only people in your organization
Description: Restrict OneDrive external sharing to existing guests or internal users only

Title: External sharing SHALL be restricted to approved external domains and/or users in approved security groups
Description: Limit external sharing to approved domains and security groups per collaboration needs

Title: File and folder default sharing scope SHALL be set to Specific people
Description: Set default sharing scope to only the people the user specifies

Title: File and folder default sharing permissions SHALL be set to view only
Description: Set default sharing permissions to view only to limit access

Title: Expiration days for Anyone links SHALL be set to 30 days or less
Description: Limit Anyone link expiration to 30 days or fewer

Title: The allowable file and folder permissions for links SHALL be set to view only
Description: Restrict Anyone link permissions to view only

Title: Reauthentication days for people who use a verification code SHALL be set to 30 days or less
Description: Require reauthentication within 30 days for verification code users

Title: External meeting participants SHOULD NOT be enabled to request control of shared desktops or windows
Description: Prevent external participants from requesting control of shared content in meetings

Title: Anonymous users SHALL NOT be enabled to start meetings
Description: Prevent anonymous users from initiating Teams meetings

Title: Anonymous users and dial-in callers SHOULD NOT be admitted automatically
Description: Require lobby admission for anonymous users and dial-in callers

Title: Internal users SHOULD be admitted automatically
Description: Allow automatic admission for internal organizational users

Title: Dial-in users SHOULD NOT be enabled to bypass the lobby
Description: Require dial-in users to wait in the lobby before being admitted

Title: Meeting recording SHOULD be disabled
Description: Disable the ability to record Teams meetings by default

Title: Record an event SHOULD NOT be set to Always record
Description: Do not configure Teams events to always record automatically

Title: External access for users SHALL only be enabled on a per-domain basis
Description: Restrict external access to specific approved domains rather than allowing all

Title: Unmanaged users SHALL NOT be enabled to initiate contact with internal users
Description: Prevent unmanaged external users from initiating contact with organizational users

Title: Internal users SHOULD NOT be enabled to initiate contact with unmanaged users
Description: Prevent internal users from initiating contact with unmanaged external users

Title: Teams email integration SHALL be disabled
Description: Disable the ability to send emails to Teams channel email addresses

Title: Agencies SHOULD only allow installation of Microsoft apps approved by the agency
Description: Restrict Microsoft Teams app installations to agency-approved Microsoft apps only

Title: Agencies SHOULD only allow installation of third-party apps approved by the agency
Description: Restrict Teams third-party app installations to agency-approved apps only

Title: Agencies SHOULD only allow installation of custom apps approved by the agency
Description: Restrict Teams custom app installations to agency-approved apps only

Title: A DLP solution SHALL be enabled
Description: Enable a Data Loss Prevention solution for Teams and ensure comparable capabilities to Microsoft native DLP

Title: The DLP solution SHALL protect PII and sensitive information as defined by the agency
Description: Configure DLP to protect PII and restrict sharing of credit card numbers TINs and SSNs in Teams

Title: Attachments included with Teams messages SHOULD be scanned for malware
Description: Enable malware scanning for attachments shared through Teams messages

Title: Users SHOULD be prevented from opening or downloading files detected as malware
Description: Block users from opening or downloading files identified as malware in Teams

Title: URL comparison with a blocklist SHOULD be enabled
Description: Enable URL blocklist comparison for links shared in Teams

Title: User click tracking SHOULD be enabled
Description: Enable tracking of user clicks on URLs shared in Teams

Title: The Publish to Web feature SHOULD be disabled
Description: Disable Publish to Web unless the agency mission requires the capability

Title: Guest user access to the Power BI tenant SHOULD be disabled
Description: Disable guest user access to Power BI unless the agency mission requires it

Title: The Invite external users to your organization feature SHOULD be disabled
Description: Disable external user invitation in Power BI unless agency mission requires it

Title: Service principals with access to APIs SHOULD be restricted to specific security groups
Description: Restrict service principal API access to designated security groups

Title: Service principals creating and using profiles SHOULD be restricted to specific security groups
Description: Restrict service principal profile creation to designated security groups

Title: ResourceKey-based authentication SHOULD be blocked
Description: Block ResourceKey-based authentication unless a specific use case merits its use

Title: Python and R interactions SHOULD be disabled
Description: Disable Python and R code execution in Power BI visuals

Title: Sensitivity labels SHOULD be enabled for Power BI
Description: Enable and employ sensitivity labels for sensitive data per enterprise data protection policies

Title: The ability to create production and sandbox environments SHALL be restricted to admins
Description: Restrict creation of production and sandbox environments to administrators only

Title: The ability to create trial environments SHALL be restricted to admins
Description: Restrict creation of trial environments to administrators only

Title: A DLP policy SHALL be created to restrict connector access in the default Power Platform environment
Description: Create DLP policy restricting connector access in the default environment

Title: Non-default environments SHOULD have at least one DLP policy affecting them
Description: Ensure non-default Power Platform environments have DLP policies applied

Title: Power Platform tenant isolation SHALL be enabled
Description: Enable tenant isolation to prevent cross-tenant data flows

Title: An inbound/outbound connection allowlist SHOULD be configured
Description: Configure allowlist for inbound and outbound cross-tenant connections

Title: Content Security Policy SHALL be enforced for model-driven and canvas Power Apps
Description: Enforce CSP headers for model-driven and canvas Power Apps

Title: The ability to create Power Pages sites SHOULD be restricted to admins
Description: Restrict Power Pages site creation to administrators only

Title: The Share with Everyone feature SHOULD be disabled
Description: Disable the Share with Everyone feature in Power Platform
